messages.content:
WITH product_quantities AS (
  SELECT product_id, SUM(quantity) as total_sold
  FROM order_items
  GROUP BY product_id
)
SELECT p.name, pq.total_sold
FROM product_quantities pq
JOIN products p ON pq.product_id = p.product_id
ORDER BY pq.total_sold DESC;

WITH gpa_stats AS (
  SELECT student_id, gpa
  FROM students
)
SELECT s.*
FROM students s
WHERE s.gpa > (SELECT AVG(gpa) FROM gpa_stats);

WITH monthly_appts AS (
  SELECT SUBSTR(date, 1, 7) as month, COUNT(*) as appt_count
  FROM appointments
  WHERE date LIKE '2024%'
  GROUP BY month
)
SELECT * FROM monthly_appts ORDER BY month;

WITH year_gpas AS (
  SELECT year, gpa
  FROM students
)
SELECT year, AVG(gpa) as avg_gpa
FROM year_gpas
GROUP BY year
ORDER BY year;

WITH student_enrollments AS (
  SELECT student_id, COUNT(*) as enrollment_count
  FROM enrollments
  GROUP BY student_id
)
SELECT s.name, se.enrollment_count
FROM student_enrollments se
JOIN students s ON se.student_id = s.student_id
ORDER BY se.enrollment_count DESC;

WITH budget_stats AS (
  SELECT dept_id, budget
  FROM departments
)
SELECT d.*
FROM departments d
WHERE d.budget > (SELECT AVG(budget) FROM budget_stats);

WITH age_groups AS (
  SELECT 
    CASE 
      WHEN dob > '2000-01-01' THEN 'Young'
      WHEN dob > '1970-01-01' THEN 'Middle'
      ELSE 'Senior'
    END as age_group
  FROM patients
)
SELECT age_group, COUNT(*) as patient_count
FROM age_groups
GROUP BY age_group;

WITH salary_stats AS (
  SELECT emp_id, salary
  FROM employees
)
SELECT e.*
FROM employees e
WHERE e.salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM salary_stats);

WITH project_hours AS (
  SELECT proj_id, SUM(hours) as total_hours
  FROM assignments
  GROUP BY proj_id
)
SELECT p.name, ph.total_hours
FROM project_hours ph
JOIN projects p ON ph.proj_id = p.proj_id
ORDER BY ph.total_hours DESC;

WITH dept_projects AS (
  SELECT d.name, COUNT(p.proj_id) as project_count
  FROM departments d
  LEFT JOIN projects p ON d.dept_id = p.dept_id
  GROUP BY d.dept_id, d.name
)
SELECT * FROM dept_projects ORDER BY project_count DESC;

WITH patient_appts AS (
  SELECT patient_id, COUNT(*) as appt_count
  FROM appointments
  GROUP BY patient_id
)
SELECT p.name, pa.appt_count
FROM patient_appts pa
JOIN patients p ON pa.patient_id = p.patient_id
ORDER BY pa.appt_count DESC;

WITH customer_spending AS (
  SELECT customer_id, SUM(total_amount) as total_spent
  FROM orders
  GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT c.name, cs.total_spent
FROM customer_spending cs
JOIN customers c ON cs.customer_id = c.customer_id
ORDER BY cs.total_spent DESC;

WITH dept_salaries AS (
  SELECT department, salary
  FROM employees
)
SELECT department, AVG(salary) as avg_salary
FROM dept_salaries
GROUP BY department
ORDER BY avg_salary DESC;

WITH doctor_workload AS (
  SELECT doctor_id, COUNT(*) as appt_count
  FROM appointments
  GROUP BY doctor_id
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 10
)
SELECT d.*, dw.appt_count
FROM doctor_workload dw
JOIN doctors d ON dw.doctor_id = d.doctor_id;

WITH order_totals AS (
  SELECT c.country, o.total_amount
  FROM orders o
  JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
)
SELECT country, AVG(total_amount) as avg_order_value
FROM order_totals
GROUP BY country;

WITH dept_credits AS (
  SELECT department, credits
  FROM courses
)
SELECT department, AVG(credits) as avg_credits
FROM dept_credits
GROUP BY department;

WITH doctor_appts AS (
  SELECT doctor_id, COUNT(*) as appt_count
  FROM appointments
  GROUP BY doctor_id
)
SELECT d.name, da.appt_count
FROM doctor_appts da
JOIN doctors d ON da.doctor_id = d.doctor_id
ORDER BY da.appt_count DESC;

WITH product_order_counts AS (
  SELECT product_id, COUNT(*) as times_ordered
  FROM order_items
  GROUP BY product_id
)
SELECT p.name, poc.times_ordered
FROM product_order_counts poc
JOIN products p ON poc.product_id = p.product_id
ORDER BY poc.times_ordered DESC;

WITH RECURSIVE org_tree AS (
  SELECT emp_id, name, manager_id, 0 as level
  FROM employees
  WHERE manager_id IS NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT e.emp_id, e.name, e.manager_id, ot.level + 1
  FROM employees e
  JOIN org_tree ot ON e.manager_id = ot.emp_id
)
SELECT * FROM org_tree;

WITH patient_appt_counts AS (
  SELECT patient_id, COUNT(*) as appt_count
  FROM appointments
  GROUP BY patient_id
)
SELECT p.*, pac.appt_count
FROM patient_appt_counts pac
JOIN patients p ON pac.patient_id = p.patient_id
WHERE pac.appt_count > (SELECT AVG(appt_count) FROM patient_appt_counts);

WITH order_counts AS (
  SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) as num_orders
  FROM orders
  GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT c.name, oc.num_orders
FROM order_counts oc
JOIN customers c ON oc.customer_id = c.customer_id
ORDER BY oc.num_orders DESC;

WITH category_sales AS (
  SELECT p.category, oi.price * oi.quantity as revenue
  FROM order_items oi
  JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
)
SELECT category, SUM(revenue) as total_revenue
FROM category_sales
GROUP BY category;

WITH major_gpas AS (
  SELECT major, gpa
  FROM students
)
SELECT major, AVG(gpa) as avg_gpa
FROM major_gpas
GROUP BY major
ORDER BY avg_gpa DESC;

WITH manager_counts AS (
  SELECT manager_id, COUNT(*) as subordinate_count
  FROM employees
  WHERE manager_id IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY manager_id
)
SELECT e.name, mc.subordinate_count
FROM manager_counts mc
JOIN employees e ON mc.manager_id = e.emp_id
ORDER BY mc.subordinate_count DESC;

WITH country_orders AS (
  SELECT c.country, o.total_amount
  FROM orders o
  JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
)
SELECT country, MAX(total_amount) as max_order
FROM country_orders
GROUP BY country;

WITH category_stock AS (
  SELECT category, stock
  FROM products
)
SELECT category, AVG(stock) as avg_stock
FROM category_stock
GROUP BY category;

WITH customer_totals AS (
  SELECT customer_id, SUM(total_amount) as total_spent
  FROM orders
  GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT c.*, ct.total_spent
FROM customer_totals ct
JOIN customers c ON ct.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE ct.total_spent > (SELECT AVG(total_spent) FROM customer_totals);

WITH category_prices AS (
  SELECT category, price
  FROM products
)
SELECT category, AVG(price) as avg_price
FROM category_prices
GROUP BY category;

WITH RECURSIVE semester_series AS (
  SELECT 2020 as year, 'Fall' as term
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 
    CASE WHEN term = 'Fall' THEN year ELSE year + 1 END,
    CASE WHEN term = 'Fall' THEN 'Spring' ELSE 'Fall' END
  FROM semester_series
  WHERE year < 2024 OR (year = 2024 AND term = 'Fall')
)
SELECT term || ' ' || year as semester FROM semester_series;

WITH RECURSIVE reporting_chain AS (
  SELECT emp_id, name, manager_id, 0 as level
  FROM employees
  WHERE emp_id = 1005
  UNION ALL
  SELECT e.emp_id, e.name, e.manager_id, rc.level + 1
  FROM employees e
  JOIN reporting_chain rc ON e.emp_id = rc.manager_id
)
SELECT * FROM reporting_chain;
